entries.definition:
for man’s very soul incites him to evil.
إمر ʾimr [quasi-act. part.] grievous, abominable, foul (18:71) لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْئًا إِمْرًا did you make a hole in it, so as to drown its passengers?, indeed, you have done a foul thing!

أمس ʾams [n.] 1 yesterday, the previous day (28:19) يَا مُوسَى أَتُرِيدُ أَنْ تَقْتُلَنِى كَمَا قَتَلْتَ نَفْسًا بِالْأَمْسِ Moses, do you want to kill me, as you killed a soul yesterday? 2 [also used adverbially] the near past, a short time ago, just recently (28:82) وَأَصْبَحَ الَّذِينَ تَمَنَّوْا مَكَانَهُ بِالْأَمْسِ يَقُولُونَ the next day saw those who had just recently wished to be in his place exclaiming, ‘Well! It is God alone who increases the provision to whoever of His servants He will.’

تمسون tumsūn (see م/س/و-ي m-s-w/y).

for man’s very soul incites him to evil.
إمر ʾimr [quasi-act. part.] grievous, abominable, foul (18:71) لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْئًا إِمْرًا did you make a hole in it, so as to drown its passengers?, indeed, you have done a foul thing!

It is God alone who increases the provision to whoever of His servants He will.’

entries.definition:
ʾ-m-m this root denotes two primary inter-related concepts of mother, race, roots and group on the one hand, and on the other, front, main road, leader, example and direction. Of this root, 10 forms occur 119 times in the Qurʾan: آمّين ʾāmīn once; أم umm 24 times; أمهات ʾummahāt 11 times; أمة ʾummatun 51 times; أمم ʾumam 13 times; أمام ʾamām once; إمام ʾimām seven times; أئمة ʾaʾimmatun five times; أمي ʾummiyy twice and أميون ʾummiyyūn four times.
آمّين ʾāmīn [pl. of act. part. آمّ ʾāmm] heading for, setting out for (5:2) وَلَا آمِّينَ الْبَيْتَ الْحَرَامَ nor heading for the Sacred House.

أم ʾumm [n.] I [pl. أمهات ʾummahāt] 1 mother (23:50) وَجَعَلْنَا ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَأُمَّهُ آيَةً and We made the son of Mary and his mother an

48
entries.definition_english:
ʾ-m-m this root denotes two primary inter-related concepts of mother, race, roots and group on the one hand, and on the other, front, main road, leader, example and direction. Of this root, 10 forms occur 119 times in the Qurʾan: آمّين ʾāmīn once; أم umm 24 times; أمهات ʾummahāt 11 times; أمة ʾummatun 51 times; أمم ʾumam 13 times; أمام ʾamām once; إمام ʾimām seven times; أئمة ʾaʾimmatun five times; أمي ʾummiyy twice and أميون ʾummiyyūn four times.
آمّين ʾāmīn [pl. of act. part. آمّ ʾāmm] heading for, setting out for (5:2) وَلَا آمِّينَ الْبَيْتَ الْحَرَامَ nor heading for the Sacred House.

أم ʾumm [n.] I [pl. أمهات ʾummahāt] 1 mother (23:50) وَجَعَلْنَا ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَأُمَّهُ آيَةً and We made the son of Mary and his mother an

48

entries.definition:
exemplar; *(20:94) يَا ابْنَ أُمَّ [an endearing form of address] son of my own mother, my own flesh and blood 2 foster mother, milk mother (4:23) حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ أُمَّهَاتُكُمْ ... وَأُمَّهَاتُكُمُ الَّاتِى أَرْضَعْنَكُمْ forbidden to you are your mothers … your milk mothers 3 (in relation to Muslims) wives of the Prophet, mothers of the believers (33:6) النَّبِىُّ أَوْلَى بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ أَنْفُسِهِمْ وَأَزْوَاجُهُ أُمَّهَاتُهُمْ the Prophet is more caring towards the believers than they are themselves, and his wives are [as] their mothers II [no plural] 1 foundation, cornerstone; canon; essence (3:7) هُوَ الَّذِى أَنْزَلَ عَلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ مِنْهُ آيَاتٌ مُحْكَمَاتٌ هُنَّ أُمُّ الْكِتَابِ وَأُخَرُ مُتَشَابِهَاتٌ it is He who has sent this Book down to you, in which are verses definite in meaning—these are the essence of the Book—and others that are ambiguous/allegorical 2 source, origin (43:4) وَإِنَّهُ فِى أُمِّ الْكِتَابِ لَدَيْنَا indeed it is [inscribed] in the original record [lit. source of the Book], kept with Us, exalted and full of wisdom 3 centre, metropolis, capital or main [city] (28:59) وَمَا كَانَ رَبُّكَ مُهْلِكَ الْقُرَى حَتَّى يَبْعَثَ فِى أُمِّهَا رَسُولًا your Lord would never destroy towns until He first raises a messenger in its main city reciting Our messages to them; *(6:92) أُمَّ الْقُرَى Mecca—mother of cities [lit. magnet of dwellings] 4 destination, final abode, permanent home (101:8–9) فَأُمُّهُ هَاوِيَةٌ but as for the one whose scales [of good deeds] are light—His final abode will be the bottomless Pit.
أمة ʾummatun [n., pl. أمم ʾumam] 1 a people, a nation, a community (35:24) وَإِنْ مِنْ أُمَّةٍ إِلَّا خَلَا فِيهَا نَذِيرٌ there has not been a community, but came to it [lit. passed away in it] a warner 2 a party, a category of people (3:104) وَلْتَكُنْ مِنْكُمْ أُمَّةٌ let there be a community from among you [also interpreted as: let you all turn out to be a community] that calls to goodness 3 a crowd (28:23) وَلَمَّا وَرَدَ مَاءَ مَدْيَنَ وَجَدَ عَلَيْهِ أُمَّةً مِنَ النَّاسِ يَسْقُونَ and when he arrived at the water of Midian, he found a crowd of people watering [their flocks] 4 a common belief, tradition (43:22) بَلْ قَالُوا إِنَّا وَجَدْنَا آبَاءَنَا عَلَى أُمَّةٍ وَإِنَّا عَلَى آثَارِهِمْ مُهْتَدُونَ no indeed!, they say, ‘We saw our fathers following this common belief; we are guided by their footsteps’ 5 a period of time (12:45) وَادَّكَرَ بَعْدَ أُمَّةٍ then the one who had gained freedom of the two, remembering [Joseph] after a while 6 a fixed, or determined time (11:8) وَلَئِنْ أَخَّرْنَا عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابَ إِلَى أُمَّةٍ مَعْدُودَةٍ if We delay the chastisement till a

49
أمّا

determined point in time 7 organised society (6:38) وَمَا مِنْ دَابَّةٍ فِى الْأَرْضِ وَلَا طَائِرٍ يَطِيرُ بِجَنَاحَيْهِ إِلَّا أُمَمٌ أَمْثَالُكُمْ [there are] no creatures on the earth, nor birds that fly with their two wings, but [that they are] [organised] communities like yourselves 8 an epitome of Godliness, a man alone, one of a kind (16:120) إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ كَانَ أُمَّةً Abraham was truly an example, an epitome of godliness.

أمام ʾamām [adverbial] front, forward direction; coming time (75:5) بَلْ يُرِيدُ الْإِنْسَانُ لِيَفْجُرَ أَمَامَهُ yet man wants to continue sinning in the future (or, Yet man wants to deny the truth about things yet to come [such as the Day of Resurrection]) [lit. yet man wants to sin ahead of him].

إمام ʾimām [n., pl. أئمة ʾaʾimmatun] 1 a leader (2:124) وَإِذِ ابْتَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ رَبُّهُ بِكَلِمَاتٍ فَأَتَمَّهُنَّ قَالَ إِنِّى جَاعِلُكَ لِلنَّاسِ إِمَامًا when Abraham’s Lord tested him with certain commandments, which he fulfilled, He said, ‘I will make you a leader of men’; *(9:12) أَئِمَّةَ الْكُفْرِ hardened disbelievers [lit. leaders of disbelief] 2 a divine writ, a guide (11:17) وَمِنْ قَبْلِهِ كِتَابُ مُوسَى إِمَامًا وَرَحْمَةً and before it, the Book of Moses, a guide and mercy 3 master record (36:12) وَكُلَّ شَىْءٍ أَحْصَيْنَاهُ فِى إِمَامٍ مُبِينٍ and We keep an account of everything in a clear Master Record 4 creed, conviction (in one interpretation of 17:71) يَوْمَ نَدْعُوا كُلَّ أُنَاسٍ بِإِمَامِهِمْ on the Day when We will call every people by their creed [also: by their leader, their book or their mothers] 5 an example, a model (25:74) وَاجْعَلْنَا لِلْمُتَّقِينَ إِمَامًا and make us good examples for the pious 6 a highway (15:79) فَانْتَقَمْنَا مِنْهُمْ وَإِنَّهُمَا لَبِإِمَامٍ مُبِينٍ and We took retribution from them; both are still there on a clearly marked highway.

أمي ʾummiyy [nisba adj./n.] 1 unlettered, unable to read (7:158) فَآمِنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ النَّبِىِّ الْأُمِّىِّ so believe in God and His Messenger, the unlettered prophet 2 a pagan, a person with no revealed scripture (3:20) وَقُلْ لِلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ وَالْأُمِّيِّينَ ask those who were given the Book, as well as those without one, ‘Do you surrender yourselves entirely to Him too?’

أمّا ʾammā, occurs 55 times in the Qurʾan and functions as: 1 a detailing (حرف تفصيل) or a partitive particle implying conditional

ّا

dependency, comparison or contrast between the two constituents of its sentence (the second of which will always be prefixed with ف) and lending emphasis to it, ‘as for … it was/is …’ (18:79) أَمَّا السَّفِينَةُ فَكَانَتْ لِمَسَاكِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ فِي الْبَحْرِ as for the boat, it belonged to poor people working on the sea 2 a conditional particle indicating comparison or contrast (أداة شرط), in which case it is usually repeated in the discourse, i.e. ‘as for …, and as for …’, as in (2:26) فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فَيَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّهُ الْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّهِمْ وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فَيَقُولُونَ مَاذَا أَرَادَ اللَّهُ بِهَٰذَا مَثَلًا as for those who believe they know it is the truth from their Lord, but as for those who do not believe they say, ‘What [ever] does God mean by such a comparison?’

إِمَّا ʾimmā, occurs 30 times in the Qur’an and functions as: 1 option particle (حرف تخيير) ‘whether … or …’ (18:86) قُلْنَا يَا ذَا الْقَرْنَيْنِ إِمَّا أَن تُعَذِّبَ وَإِمَّا أَن تَتَّخِذَ فِيهِمْ حُسْنًا We said, ‘Dhū’l-Qarnayn, you may choose whether to punish or show them kindness’ 2 detailing or partitive particle (حرف تفصيل) (76:3) إِنَّا هَدَيْنَاهُ السَّبِيلَ إِمَّا شَاكِرًا وَإِمَّا كَفُورًا We guided him to the [right] way; he is either thankful or ungrateful 3 a particle denoting uncertainty of an outcome (9:106) وَآخَرُونَ مُرْجَوْنَ لِأَمْرِ اللَّهِ إِمَّا يُعَذِّبُهُمْ وَإِمَّا يَتُوبُ عَلَيْهِمْ and others are deferred to God’s decree, whether He chastises them or accepts their repentance. Differentiation is made between the above إِمَّا and the one in (19:26) فَإِمَّا تَرَيِنَّ مِنَ الْبَشَرِ أَحَدًا فَقُولِي إِنِّي نَذَرْتُ لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ صَوْمًا and if you see any human being, you say: ‘I have vowed to the Lord of Mercy a fast’. The latter is a compound of conditional إِن + so-called ‘redundant’ or emphatic mā (ما).
entries.definition_english:
exemplar; *(20:94) يَا ابْنَ أُمَّ [an endearing form of address] son of my own mother, my own flesh and blood 2 foster mother, milk mother (4:23) حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ أُمَّهَاتُكُمْ ... وَأُمَّهَاتُكُمُ الَّاتِى أَرْضَعْنَكُمْ forbidden to you are your mothers … your milk mothers 3 (in relation to Muslims) wives of the Prophet, mothers of the believers (33:6) النَّبِىُّ أَوْلَى بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ أَنْفُسِهِمْ وَأَزْوَاجُهُ أُمَّهَاتُهُمْ the Prophet is more caring towards the believers than they are themselves, and his wives are [as] their mothers II [no plural] 1 foundation, cornerstone; canon; essence (3:7) هُوَ الَّذِى أَنْزَلَ عَلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ مِنْهُ آيَاتٌ مُحْكَمَاتٌ هُنَّ أُمُّ الْكِتَابِ وَأُخَرُ مُتَشَابِهَاتٌ it is He who has sent this Book down to you, in which are verses definite in meaning—these are the essence of the Book—and others that are ambiguous/allegorical 2 source, origin (43:4) وَإِنَّهُ فِى أُمِّ الْكِتَابِ لَدَيْنَا indeed it is [inscribed] in the original record [lit. source of the Book], kept with Us, exalted and full of wisdom 3 centre, metropolis, capital or main [city] (28:59) وَمَا كَانَ رَبُّكَ مُهْلِكَ الْقُرَى حَتَّى يَبْعَثَ فِى أُمِّهَا رَسُولًا your Lord would never destroy towns until He first raises a messenger in its main city reciting Our messages to them; *(6:92) أُمَّ الْقُرَى Mecca—mother of cities [lit. magnet of dwellings] 4 destination, final abode, permanent home (101:8–9) فَأُمُّهُ هَاوِيَةٌ but as for the one whose scales [of good deeds] are light—His final abode will be the bottomless Pit.
أمة ʾummatun [n., pl. أمم ʾumam] 1 a people, a nation, a community (35:24) وَإِنْ مِنْ أُمَّةٍ إِلَّا خَلَا فِيهَا نَذِيرٌ there has not been a community, but came to it [lit. passed away in it] a warner 2 a party, a category of people (3:104) وَلْتَكُنْ مِنْكُمْ أُمَّةٌ let there be a community from among you [also interpreted as: let you all turn out to be a community] that calls to goodness 3 a crowd (28:23) وَلَمَّا وَرَدَ مَاءَ مَدْيَنَ وَجَدَ عَلَيْهِ أُمَّةً مِنَ النَّاسِ يَسْقُونَ and when he arrived at the water of Midian, he found a crowd of people watering [their flocks] 4 a common belief, tradition (43:22) بَلْ قَالُوا إِنَّا وَجَدْنَا آبَاءَنَا عَلَى أُمَّةٍ وَإِنَّا عَلَى آثَارِهِمْ مُهْتَدُونَ no indeed!, they say, ‘We saw our fathers following this common belief; we are guided by their footsteps’ 5 a period of time (12:45) وَادَّكَرَ بَعْدَ أُمَّةٍ then the one who had gained freedom of the two, remembering [Joseph] after a while 6 a fixed, or determined time (11:8) وَلَئِنْ أَخَّرْنَا عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابَ إِلَى أُمَّةٍ مَعْدُودَةٍ if We delay the chastisement till a

49
أمّا

determined point in time 7 organised society (6:38) وَمَا مِنْ دَابَّةٍ فِى الْأَرْضِ وَلَا طَائِرٍ يَطِيرُ بِجَنَاحَيْهِ إِلَّا أُمَمٌ أَمْثَالُكُمْ [there are] no creatures on the earth, nor birds that fly with their two wings, but [that they are] [organised] communities like yourselves 8 an epitome of Godliness, a man alone, one of a kind (16:120) إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ كَانَ أُمَّةً Abraham was truly an example, an epitome of godliness.

أمام ʾamām [adverbial] front, forward direction; coming time (75:5) بَلْ يُرِيدُ الْإِنْسَانُ لِيَفْجُرَ أَمَامَهُ yet man wants to continue sinning in the future (or, Yet man wants to deny the truth about things yet to come [such as the Day of Resurrection]) [lit. yet man wants to sin ahead of him].

إمام ʾimām [n., pl. أئمة ʾaʾimmatun] 1 a leader (2:124) وَإِذِ ابْتَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ رَبُّهُ بِكَلِمَاتٍ فَأَتَمَّهُنَّ قَالَ إِنِّى جَاعِلُكَ لِلنَّاسِ إِمَامًا when Abraham’s Lord tested him with certain commandments, which he fulfilled, He said, ‘I will make you a leader of men’; *(9:12) أَئِمَّةَ الْكُفْرِ hardened disbelievers [lit. leaders of disbelief] 2 a divine writ, a guide (11:17) وَمِنْ قَبْلِهِ كِتَابُ مُوسَى إِمَامًا وَرَحْمَةً and before it, the Book of Moses, a guide and mercy 3 master record (36:12) وَكُلَّ شَىْءٍ أَحْصَيْنَاهُ فِى إِمَامٍ مُبِينٍ and We keep an account of everything in a clear Master Record 4 creed, conviction (in one interpretation of 17:71) يَوْمَ نَدْعُوا كُلَّ أُنَاسٍ بِإِمَامِهِمْ on the Day when We will call every people by their creed [also: by their leader, their book or their mothers] 5 an example, a model (25:74) وَاجْعَلْنَا لِلْمُتَّقِينَ إِمَامًا and make us good examples for the pious 6 a highway (15:79) فَانْتَقَمْنَا مِنْهُمْ وَإِنَّهُمَا لَبِإِمَامٍ مُبِينٍ and We took retribution from them; both are still there on a clearly marked highway.

أمي ʾummiyy [nisba adj./n.] 1 unlettered, unable to read (7:158) فَآمِنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ النَّبِىِّ الْأُمِّىِّ so believe in God and His Messenger, the unlettered prophet 2 a pagan, a person with no revealed scripture (3:20) وَقُلْ لِلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ وَالْأُمِّيِّينَ ask those who were given the Book, as well as those without one, ‘Do you surrender yourselves entirely to Him too?’

أمّا ʾammā, occurs 55 times in the Qurʾan and functions as: 1 a detailing (حرف تفصيل) or a partitive particle implying conditional

ّا

dependency, comparison or contrast between the two constituents of its sentence (the second of which will always be prefixed with ف) and lending emphasis to it, ‘as for … it was/is …’ (18:79) أَمَّا السَّفِينَةُ فَكَانَتْ لِمَسَاكِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ فِي الْبَحْرِ as for the boat, it belonged to poor people working on the sea 2 a conditional particle indicating comparison or contrast (أداة شرط), in which case it is usually repeated in the discourse, i.e. ‘as for …, and as for …’, as in (2:26) فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فَيَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّهُ الْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّهِمْ وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فَيَقُولُونَ مَاذَا أَرَادَ اللَّهُ بِهَٰذَا مَثَلًا as for those who believe they know it is the truth from their Lord, but as for those who do not believe they say, ‘What [ever] does God mean by such a comparison?’

إِمَّا ʾimmā, occurs 30 times in the Qur’an and functions as: 1 option particle (حرف تخيير) ‘whether … or …’ (18:86) قُلْنَا يَا ذَا الْقَرْنَيْنِ إِمَّا أَن تُعَذِّبَ وَإِمَّا أَن تَتَّخِذَ فِيهِمْ حُسْنًا We said, ‘Dhū’l-Qarnayn, you may choose whether to punish or show them kindness’ 2 detailing or partitive particle (حرف تفصيل) (76:3) إِنَّا هَدَيْنَاهُ السَّبِيلَ إِمَّا شَاكِرًا وَإِمَّا كَفُورًا We guided him to the [right] way; he is either thankful or ungrateful 3 a particle denoting uncertainty of an outcome (9:106) وَآخَرُونَ مُرْجَوْنَ لِأَمْرِ اللَّهِ إِمَّا يُعَذِّبُهُمْ وَإِمَّا يَتُوبُ عَلَيْهِمْ and others are deferred to God’s decree, whether He chastises them or accepts their repentance. Differentiation is made between the above إِمَّا and the one in (19:26) فَإِمَّا تَرَيِنَّ مِنَ الْبَشَرِ أَحَدًا فَقُولِي إِنِّي نَذَرْتُ لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ صَوْمًا and if you see any human being, you say: ‘I have vowed to the Lord of Mercy a fast’. The latter is a compound of conditional إِن + so-called ‘redundant’ or emphatic mā (ما).
